mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. This signal is always active as retail sales data is continuously released.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward returns show a negative correlation with RSAFS changes (Spearman rank correlation < -0.10) over a 2-year period, or the signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over two months signals a tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.3 pp over 2 months
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the 5-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near zero and core inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to rise, real interest rates turn negative, incentivizing equity investment over cash and bonds, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. The signal requires joint behavior of low policy rates and rising inflation.
failure_condition: In quarters where the trigger activates, the 60-day SPX return is not positive on average or the positive instances are less than 60% of all triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicate a healing labor market that drives consumer spending and lifts equities. This signal is only effective in the high-unemployment regime, as payroll additions in a full-employment environment have different implications.
failure_condition: Within the regime, the correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.10, or the hit rate is below 55% over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% signals a recovery in the residential real estate market, boosting construction employment and related sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered events result in negative 5-day returns more than half the time, or the average return is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) increases the discount rate on future cash flows, reduces equity valuations, and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon. This is a continuous always-on signal based on the trend in rates.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns exhibit a positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Spearman > 0.10) over any 3-year window, or the signal's directional accuracy is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) both increase month-over-month simultaneously, it signals a broad-based economic expansion with rising output and employment, which is robustly positive for S&P 500 returns over a 20-trading-day horizon. The joint signal is stronger than either alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change > 0% and PAYEMS monthly change > 0%
failure_condition: In months where both proxies are positive, the 20-day forward SPX return is not positive on average or the win rate is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A robust increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) of more than 100,000 jobs in a single month signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show negative 20-day returns more than 45% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive.
